articles.description: Women's Health editors discuss the news on the latest episode of The Huddle podcast.

articles.title: Former state Sen. Paula Hollinger dies at age 85
articles.description: Former state Sen. Paula Hollinger, a Baltimore County Democrat known as a staunch advocate for women's health and reproductive rights during nearly 30 years in the General Assembly, died Wednesday at age 85.

articles.title: 'Ready or Not 2' Actor Samara Weaving Relies on This One Relaxing Ritual to Recover After Action Scenes
articles.description: "I think that's what carried me through," she tells Women's Health in the latest episode of Well Said.

articles.title: 30+ Best Fitness Gifts of 2026, According to Women's Health Editors
articles.description: Trust us, your wellness-obsessed friend will be thrilled.

articles.title: Maryland Higher Education secretary leaving after three years in job
articles.description: Maryland Higher Education Secretary Sanjay Rai is stepping down Friday, his last day leading the agency that oversees statewide policies on public and private colleges and universities.

articles.title: Dead for 3.6 million years, but megalodon still survives another day in the legislature
articles.description: The giant prehistoric shark megalodon moved one step closer to becoming Maryland's official state shark Monday, one of a handful of commemorative bills that won final approval from one chamber or the other in recent days

articles.title: <![CDATA[Retatrutide Shows Reductions in Glycated Hemoglobin and Body Weight in Phase 3 Trial]]>
articles.description: <![CDATA[Lilly’s once-weekly retatrutide lowers A1C up to 2% and drives nearly 17% weight loss in type 2 diabetes, signaling a powerful triple-agonist shift.]]>

articles.title: Contractors, customers worried by cuts to Maryland energy efficiency rebates
articles.description: As state lawmakers move toward short-term reductions to the EmPOWER program, which helps homeowners pay for energy-saving improvements, residents and the businesses that rely on the program try to figure out what's next.

articles.title: One part of Maryland’s plan for cleaning its air? A more leafy state
articles.description: Maryland was 1.5 million trees on the way to its goal of planting more than 5 million native trees across the state by the end of 2031 to help reduce carbon in the atmosphere, under the state's Tree Solutions Now Act of

articles.title: Supreme Court of Maryland restores Bereano’s law license, 26 years later
articles.description: The Supreme Court of Maryland restored the law license Friday of Annapolis power lobbyist Bruce Bereano, more than 26 years after it disbarred him in the wake of a federal mail fraud conviction linked to his lobbying fir

articles.title: HHS to investigate 13 states that require insurers to cover abortions
articles.description: Maryland is reportedly one of 13 states that is being investigated by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, because the states require health insurance plans to cover abortion care.

articles.title: Advocates could begin recruiting appointees for long-awaited Reparations Commission soon
articles.description: Supporters could begin soliciting volunteers to serve on the long-awaited, and long-delayed Maryland Reparations Commission as soon as next week, ending a year of starts and stops on the measure.

articles.title: Money doesn’t grow on trees, but nature pays dividends in Maryland
articles.description: Programs like Open Space are efficient, effective investments in the health and beauty and livability of Maryland, writes Allison Colden, and are worth defending even in lean budget times.

articles.title: Bill bans those convicted in the Jan. 6 attack on U.S. Capitol from serving on Maryland boards

articles.title: ‘No Kings Act’ passes committee, heads to full Senate for consideration
articles.description: A Senate panel approved a bill to let Marylanders sue federal agents in state courts for constitutional violations, despite concerns by one Republican lawmaker who called it just “another slap at federal law enforcement”

articles.title: Broad energy bill passes Maryland House with bipartisan support
articles.description: The House gave overwhelming approval Tuesday night to a broad piece of energy legislation that Democrats believe will lead to a noticeable reduction in peoples' gas and electric bills, starting as soon as this summer.

articles.title: Maryland’s moment: Protecting Marylanders from high drug costs

articles.title: Pain of soaring gas prices compounded by electricity rate increases across states

articles.title: Sewer limits force development moratorium in Baltimore suburbs
articles.description: A portion of Anne Arundel County, including a shopping complex and business district near BWI, has been put under a growth moratorium to control the threat of more wet-weather sewage overflows into the Patapsco River.

articles.title: Songs, sharks and Charlie Kirk: Commemorative bills are many, but few are passed into law

articles.title: Maryland religious leaders call for continuing conversations after Michigan synagogue attack

articles.title: Maryland says ‘Leave no one behind.’ So, why are young adults still invisible?
articles.description: When it comes to inclusion for young adults with chronic conditions, Maryland has studied the issue, documented it and adopted policies on it, writes Santi Bhagat. But it lags when it comes to executing, leaving many "Yo

articles.title: Energy legislation would save Marylanders at least $150 annually on bills, lawmakers say
articles.description: Maryland households will see at least $150 a year in utility bill savings from a package of bills unveiled late this week, the governor and legislative leaders promised Friday.

articles.title: Ground stop at BWI, DC-area airports lifted after ‘strong chemical smell’ halted operations
articles.description: Baltimore/Washington International Airport and both D.C.-area airports were shut down for hours Friday by a ground stop that was ordered after a “strong chemical smell” at a Federal Aviation Administration facility in Vi

articles.title: ‘Cash is the staple of our country’: Bill would push businesses to accept cash
articles.description: State lawmakers are considering a bill that would require businesses to accept cash payments for purchases less than $300, a move that supporters said keeps the economy open to all Marylanders.

articles.title: Former Lt. Gov. Melvin “Mickey” Steinberg, dies at age 92
articles.description: Melvin A. "Mickey" Steinberg, Maryland's fifth lieutenant governor and a former Senate president known for his political acumen and sense of humor, died Tuesday. He was 92.

articles.title: Tale of two budget years adds $250 million to revenue projections
articles.description: Maryland's revenue picture improved by nearly $250 million over two years, buoyed largely by a factor that can't be anticipated — people dying. The numbers from the Board of Revenue Estimates also confirm a sharp drop in

articles.title: Maryland families cannot be hit twice by disability service cuts
articles.description: For thousands of Marylanders and their families who rely on support from the Developmental Disabilities Administration, last year's budget cuts had real impact, write Ande Kolp and Laura Howell, and a second year of cuts

articles.title: Democrats field candidates in every legislative race in Maryland for the first time since 1974
articles.description: For the first time since 1974, Maryland Democrats say they have fielded a candidate to run for every legislative seat in the state this fall, in a "compete everywhere" strategy that hopes to capitalize on dissatisfaction

articles.title: Maryland ramps up legal action against federal immigration facilities
articles.description: Maryland doubled down on its legal challenge to federal immigration policy Tuesday, suing for records in its probe of conditions at a Baltimore detention center while seeking an emergency order to stop construction at a

articles.title: Half of Maryland’s school districts still not at $60K teacher salary threshold

articles.title: Political ‘flag’ football, Cap’n Bobby claims credit and Ellis can be quiet, in political notes

articles.title: Rockfish regulations on hold at General Assembly committee
articles.description: A legislative committee is holding up new rockfish regulations from the Maryland Department of Natural Resources that would open a catch-and-release fishery in April for the first time in several years.
